title: Pre-fetch Flickr CDN URLs into Media Thumbnail column
description: Write a one-time data prep script that batch-fetches noembed.com for all Flickr URLs in the Media column, with a ~1 second delay between requests to avoid rate limiting. Write the returned media_url (direct Flickr CDN .jpg URL) into the Media Thumbnail column in the xlsx. Save progress as it goes so it can be resumed if interrupted. When complete, regenerate timeline-data.js. This eliminates all noembed network requests from the live app — drawer images load instantly from the CDN URL. At ~1 req/sec: 516 events ≈ 9 min, 3000 events ≈ 50 min.

title: CV Storybox: embed media URLs (Vimeo/YouTube/Flickr/SoundCloud) for CV entries
description:
When a CV entry has a media URL in its description or a dedicated media field, embed it at the top of the Storybox drawer — same renderMedia() logic already used for AbTeC events.

Supported URLs to handle: Vimeo, YouTube, Flickr, SoundCloud, and any other embeddable media already supported by renderMedia().

Use the Documentaries group as the primary test case (entries have Vimeo/YouTube URLs).

Sub-task also noted: remove 'Obx Labs.' prefix/label from Documentaries entries in generate_cv_xlsx.py or fetch_cv_from_gdoc.py so headlines are cleaner.

title: CV Insights: visualize research career evolution using Claude API
description:
Use enriched CV data to add new timeline view modes that reveal the evolution of JEL's research career over time.

## Goal
Build on the existing CV Google Doc parser to produce multiple complementary views of career evolution — by research theme, concept threads, and collaborator network — rather than just by CV section.

## Architecture
**Branch:** Timeline-CV-Insights (new, branched from Timeline-AbTeC-Media)

**Step 1 — enrich_cv.py (Python + Claude API)**
- Reads CV entries from fetch_cv_from_gdoc.py output (or cv-data.js)
- For entries with {text} Dropbox links, fetches + extracts PDF text via pypdf2/pdfminer (optional phase)
- Sends batches to Claude API to extract:
  - Research themes (e.g. 'Indigenous digital sovereignty', 'AI & creativity', 'electronic literature')
  - Key concepts / keywords
  - Collaborator names
- Writes cv-data-enriched.js with extra fields: themes[], concepts[], collaborators[]

**Step 2 — New view modes in timeline sidebar**
- Sections (current — no enrichment needed)
- Themes: rows regrouped by research theme; entries can appear in multiple rows
- Concepts: one row per major concept, dots showing when it appears
- Collaborators: one row per recurring collaborator, showing when you worked together

## Prerequisites
- Anthropic API key (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var) — user does not currently have one
- API cost: one-time batch analysis of ~650 entries, estimated a few dollars total
- pypdf2 or pdfminer for PDF text extraction (optional, can skip and use CV text only)

## Notes
- PDF fetching from Dropbox is CORS-blocked in-browser; must be done in Python pipeline
- Enrichment is a one-time (or occasional) offline step; output is a static .js file
- View modes beyond Sections are disabled/hidden until enriched data is loaded

title: Auto-sync Google Sheets via GitHub Actions
description: Set up a scheduled GitHub Action that fetches the Google Sheets CSV, runs make_data_js.py (adapted to read from CSV URL), commits and pushes updated timeline-data.js automatically. Configurable schedule (e.g. hourly) plus manual trigger. Eliminates need for team members to manually reparse after editing the shared spreadsheet.

title: Mobile-friendly layout and touch controls
description: Make the timeline usable on mobile devices. Recommended approach: (1) touch pan/zoom — replace mouse drag+scroll with one-finger pan and pinch-to-zoom; (2) collapsible sidebar — hide behind hamburger button on narrow screens, slide in as overlay. Stretch goal: responsive CSS layout stacking sidebar above canvas in portrait mode. Full mobile redesign (vertical list view) is a longer-term option.
